_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Marvell Technology (MRVL). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: TTM through Q1 FY2027 (ended ~Apr 26, 2026). Q1 FY2027 from SEC 8-K filed 2026-05-27 ($2.418B revenue, $0.80 non-GAAP EPS). Q2-Q4 FY2026 from prior SEC filings. Balance sheet from FY2026 annual (Jan 31, 2026). Stock price: AH close May 27 post-earnings ($214.59).
_note: MRVL fiscal year ends January. TTM = Q2FY26($2,010M)+Q3FY26($2,075M)+Q4FY26($2,219M)+Q1FY27($2,418M)=$8,722M. GAAP EBIT deeply negative due to acquired intangibles amortization (~$639M/yr Inphi/Cavium) + SBC. Non-GAAP op margin 35% in FY2026. FY2027 mgmt guidance: ~$11B (>30% YoY). FY2028 mgmt guidance: $16.5B (~50% YoY from $11B — corrected from $15B recorded in Session 28; confirmed by Sherwood/TIKR/SeekingAlpha/Motley Fool transcript). Q4 FY2027 exit rate >$3B. June 2 Computex keynote: Jensen + Murphy confirmed NVLink Fusion integration. Google assembles 4-partner chip supply chain (Broadcom/MediaTek/Marvell/Intel); Marvell in talks for MPU + inference TPU (no signed contract). Stock June 1 close $219.26.
story_narrative: Marvell is the second wave of AI silicon — the custom ASIC choke point for hyperscaler compute and the emerging full-stack connectivity platform for AI data centers. Q1 FY2027 actuals (2026-05-27): $2.418B revenue (+28% YoY, beat $2.40B guide), non-GAAP EPS $0.80 (beat $0.79). Q2 FY2027 guided $2.7B (+35% YoY). FY2027 management guidance ~$11B (>30% YoY). FY2028 management guidance $16.5B (~50% YoY — corrected from $15B; confirmed Sherwood/TIKR/Seeking Alpha). Q4 FY2027 exit >$3B. Custom ASIC: $1.5B annual run rate FY2026 (18 cloud-provider design wins), >$3B FY2028. Confirmed/active: Google (inference TPU + MPU talks; 4-partner supply chain with Broadcom/MediaTek/Marvell/Intel; 35M Google TPUs by 2028), Amazon (Trainium; Wells Fargo $6B/yr from $225B backlog), Microsoft (Maia). Three connectivity acquisition layers: Celestial AI ($3.25B Photonic Fabric), Polariton (plasmonics), XConn ($540M PCIe 6/CXL 3.1). June 2 Computex: Jensen Huang + Matt Murphy jointly confirmed NVLink Fusion integration — Marvell custom XPUs embedded in NVIDIA AI factory ecosystem. Hyperscaler CapEx $725B 2026 (+77% YoY). NVDA $2B invested March 2026. Stock June 1 close $219.26. MoS -42.2% at $219.26 — SELL/AVOID. Risk: Northland flags CY2027 hyperscaler cash constraints; Goldman sole bear at $125 PT; Google MPU/inference TPU talks not yet contracted.
story_revenue_cagr: 25% reflects rapid ramp confirmed by Q1 FY2027 actuals and management's own FY2028 roadmap ($16.5B — corrected from $15B). Year 1 (FY2027) tracking ~$11B (+26%), Year 2 (FY2028) guided $16.5B (+50%) — front-loaded well above the 10-year average; model is conservative vs. management trajectory in years 1–3. 25% 10-year average requires sustained program wins through FY2030+. FY2028 $16.5B implies ~$4.1B avg quarterly revenue — requires meaningful sequential ramp from Q4 FY2027 exit >$3B.
story_target_margin: 32% target EBIT margin (on GAAP+R&D-cap basis). GAAP EBIT is currently distorted by large SBC and acquired intangibles amortization (~$639M/yr from Inphi/Cavium acquisitions). Non-GAAP operating margin is already 35% in FY2026. As AI data center mix grows and legacy enterprise/carrier declines, GAAP margin expands toward target. 32% is achievable if SBC normalizes as a % of revenue as the company matures.
story_growth_years: 10 years — custom ASIC program cycles are 2–3 years each; multiple hyperscaler programs staggered. Google TPU+MPU + Amazon Trainium + new wins (Microsoft, Meta) staggered through FY2028-FY2032.
story_stable_growth: 3% stable growth. Custom ASIC is not commodity; Marvell maintains design relationships but must re-win each generation. Long-term growth tied to AI infrastructure buildout durability.
story_stable_margin: 28% — mature custom ASIC design house margin on GAAP+R&D-cap basis. Higher than commodity fabless but below pure IP model. Acquisition amortization fully run off by terminal year.
story_s2c_high: 2.0x — fabless model, but R&D intensive per custom design. R&D already capitalized in model via rd_amortization_years. Each ASIC program requires significant upfront engineering investment.
story_s2c_stable: 2.2x — slight efficiency improvement as standard design platforms (SerDes IP, optical DSP) are reused across multiple customers.
story_beta: 1.55 — higher beta than TSMC/ASML because Marvell's revenue depends on both hyperscaler CapEx cycles and successful program execution. Less visibility than TSMC foundry backlog. NVDA $2B investment and analyst consensus partially reduce uncertainty.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. Custom ASIC IP has a useful life tied to the program cycle (~2–3 years) but cumulative SerDes, optical DSP, and EDA tool investments persist longer. Aligns with Damodaran semiconductor R&D amortization convention.
story_uncertainty: High — execution risk on custom ASIC programs, customer concentration (top 3 hyperscalers = >60% of AI revenue), transition from legacy business, and large GAAP vs non-GAAP divergence from acquisition accounting. May 27 earnings will be key signal.
